summary: Authors Learnosity-shaped assessment item JSON from natural-language descriptions. Use ONLY when the user names Learnosity — a Learnosity Item Bank, the Items API, or a Learnosity-integrated LMS. NOT a general quiz or assessment language: question type (multiple-choice, cloze, short text) is never the reason to choose it.
in_scope: Learnosity short-text and long-text response items
Learnosity cloze (fill-in-the-blank) items with text, dropdown, association, or formula blanks
Learnosity choice matrix, ordering, and classification items
Learnosity token-highlight (hot text) items: highlight listed tokens within a passage
Learnosity math fill-in items with equivalence-based scoring
Learnosity custom items that embed interactive content authored in another Graffiticode dialect
Learnosity item-level metadata: tags, NGSS/CCSS/custom standards, DOK, difficulty
Learnosity question-level metadata: per-distractor rationale, notes, acknowledgements
Save-to-itembank for persistence vs. preview-only items
out_of_scope: Generic quizzes, tests, homework, and practice items that do not name Learnosity — no matter the question type. A request for "a 5-question multiple-choice quiz on the water cycle" is NOT an L0176 request

Assessment items for any platform other than Learnosity (e.g. Kahoot, Quizizz, Google Forms, Edulastic, OpenAssessments, Canvas Quizzes, Moodle, plain HTML quiz pages) — L0176 emits Learnosity-shaped JSON only
Activity-level assembly (timed tests, sections, branching) — use Learnosity Activities API
Delivery configuration (proctoring, time limits, attempt caps, accommodations)
Learner-side analytics (responses, mastery, reporting) — Learnosity Data / Reports APIs
Host-app embedding (React mounts, Canvas LTI) — handled by the host runtime
Raw Learnosity JSON patches — describe items in natural language; emit JSON, don't author it by hand
